privacyPolicy.title: Privacy Policy

intro.title: Introduction
intro.content: At YO Dijital Medya A.Ş. ("YoAi", "we"), we take the security of your personal data seriously. This policy explains what data we collect when you use the YoAi platform, how we use it, how long we keep it, and your rights.

privacyPolicy.section1Content: This policy applies to the services provided through the YoAi website and YoAi SaaS dashboard.
privacyPolicy.section2Content: YoAi is a platform that helps users connect their ad accounts to view performance data, run reporting, and (where applicable) perform certain ad management actions.
privacyPolicy.section3Title: 3) Connected Integrations and Types of Data Accessed
privacyPolicy.section3MetaContent:
When a user connects their Meta account via Facebook Login, YoAi requests access to the following categories of data, each tied to a specific permission scope:

(a) Advertising Data (ads_read, ads_management, pages_manage_ads): Ad account identifiers, campaigns, ad sets, ads, budgets, bid strategies, targeting settings, and performance metrics (impressions, clicks, spend, conversions, ROAS).

(b) Page Data (pages_show_list, pages_read_engagement, pages_manage_posts): Facebook Page names, IDs, profile pictures, engagement metrics, published posts (text, images, videos, permalinks), and the ability to publish content (feed posts, Reels) to the user's own Facebook Page.

(c) Lead Data (leads_retrieval): Active lead generation forms associated with connected Pages, including form names, field configurations, and submitted lead entries (name, email, phone, and other fields defined by the advertiser).

(d) Business Data (business_management): Business Manager account identifiers and names, owned Pages, and owned WhatsApp Business Accounts — used to map organizational assets.

(e) Instagram Data (instagram_basic, instagram_content_publish): Instagram Business Account profile information (username, profile picture), published media (images, videos, captions, permalinks), and the ability to publish content (feed posts, Reels, Stories) to the user's Instagram Business Account.

(f) WhatsApp Data (whatsapp_business_management, whatsapp_business_messaging): WhatsApp Business Account identifiers, associated phone numbers (display number, verified name, quality rating), and the ability to create Click-to-WhatsApp (CTWA) ad destinations.

All Meta data is used exclusively to provide the ad management, reporting, and campaign creation features within the YoAi platform. Data is not transferred to third parties, sold, or used for purposes unrelated to the user's advertising operations.
privacyPolicy.section3GoogleContent: The user grants access to their Google Ads account via Google OAuth. Data accessed includes Google Ads customer account ID, ad entities such as campaigns/ad groups/ads, budget/settings, and performance metrics (e.g. impressions, clicks, cost, and related reporting fields). Access scope: Google Ads API (adwords scope).
privacyPolicy.section3GA4Content:
When a user connects their Google account, YoAi requests access to Google Analytics 4 data via the following OAuth scopes:

(a) analytics.readonly: Read access to GA4 account identifiers, property configurations, data streams, audiences, and report data (sessions, users, events, conversions, traffic sources).

(b) analytics.edit: Write access to create GA4 properties on behalf of the user, configure data streams, define custom event definitions, create audiences, and set up conversion goals — only when the user explicitly initiates these actions through YoAi's setup wizards.

All Google Analytics data is used exclusively to display analytics dashboards within YoAi and to perform user-initiated configuration actions. Data is never transferred to third parties, sold, or used for advertising purposes.
privacyPolicy.section3GTMContent:
When a user connects their Google account, YoAi requests access to Google Tag Manager data via the following OAuth scopes:

(a) tagmanager.readonly: Read access to the user's GTM account identifiers, containers, workspaces, tags, triggers, variables, and version history — used to display the user's existing setup within YoAi's dashboard.

(b) tagmanager.edit.containers: Write access to create and modify tags (GA4 configuration, GA4 events, conversion tracking), triggers (page view, form submit, click events), and variables within the user's GTM container — only when the user explicitly initiates these actions through YoAi's setup wizards.

(c) tagmanager.publish: Permission to publish GTM container versions, only after the user reviews and approves YoAi-generated changes via in-app one-click action. Auto-publishing is never performed without explicit user action.

All Google Tag Manager data is used exclusively to display the user's GTM setup within YoAi and to deploy tags configured by the user. Data is never transferred to third parties or used for advertising purposes.
privacyPolicy.section4SubATitle: 4.1 Google API Services User Data Policy (Limited Use)
privacyPolicy.section4SubAContent: YoAi's use and transfer to any other app of information received from Google APIs will adhere to the Google API Services User Data Policy (https://developers.google.com/terms/api-services-user-data-policy), including the Limited Use requirements. Specifically: (a) Google user data is used only to provide and improve the reporting, monitoring, and campaign management features requested by the user within YoAi. (b) Google user data is not transferred to third parties except as necessary to provide or improve user-facing features, with the user's explicit consent, for security purposes, or to comply with applicable laws. (c) Google user data is not used for serving ads, including retargeting, personalized advertising, or interest-based advertising. (d) Google user data is not sold to any party. (e) Humans are not allowed to read Google user data unless the user has provided affirmative consent, it is necessary for security purposes, it is required to comply with applicable law, or the data is aggregated and anonymized for internal operations.
privacyPolicy.section4SubBContent:
YoAi's use of data received from Meta Platform APIs (Facebook, Instagram, WhatsApp) complies with the Meta Platform Terms (https://developers.facebook.com/terms/) and Meta's Data Use Restrictions. Specifically:

(a) Meta user data is used solely to provide the advertising management, reporting, campaign creation, and analytics features that the user explicitly initiates within the YoAi platform.

(b) Meta user data is not sold, licensed, or transferred to any third party — including data brokers, ad networks, or analytics providers — for any purpose.

(c) Meta user data is not used for surveillance, profiling unrelated to advertising, credit or insurance eligibility decisions, employment screening, or any purpose unrelated to the user's own advertising operations.

(d) Lead form data retrieved via the leads_retrieval permission is accessed only on behalf of the Page owner who created the form and is displayed exclusively within the YoAi dashboard for that user's own use.

(e) Instagram account data is used only for campaign targeting (selecting Instagram accounts as ad destinations) and displaying published media for ad creative selection.

(f) WhatsApp Business Account data is used only to enable Click-to-WhatsApp ad creation and to verify phone number availability.

(g) Access tokens are stored securely in httpOnly cookies and server-side storage. Tokens are never exposed to client-side JavaScript or logged in plain text.

(h) When a user disconnects their Meta integration or deauthorizes the app, all stored tokens, account mappings, and cached data are revoked and queued for deletion within 90 days.

(i) Users may request immediate data deletion by contacting info@yodijital.com or through the in-app data deletion flow.
privacyPolicy.section5Content: Account linking and authentication; providing performance reports, KPI dashboards, and analytics; in-product debugging and support (sensitive information such as credit card, password, or tokens is not logged); security measures and prevention of misuse.
privacyPolicy.section6Content: Reporting/cache data: 90 days (to the minimum extent necessary for functionality). When the user removes an integration (Disconnect), synchronization is stopped and the related authentication data is revoked; integration data held in the system is subject to deletion procedures.
privacyPolicy.section7Content: Data is not sold to third parties. Transfer to infrastructure providers (hosting, logging, etc.) may occur only to the limited extent necessary to provide the service. Disclosure to competent authorities may occur where required by law.
privacyPolicy.section8Content: Tokens and sensitive authentication data are not held in plain form on the client. Access keys are not shared in URLs; they are used only in secure headers. Technical and organizational measures are in place to prevent unauthorized access.
privacyPolicy.section9Content:
You may submit requests regarding the processing of your personal data through the following channel:

Email: info@yodijital.com
privacyPolicy.section10Content: This policy may be updated as needed. Updates will be published on this page.

dataDeletion.title: Data Deletion and Integration Removal

intro.title: Introduction
intro.content: You may remove integrations you have connected in YoAi at any time and request deletion of your data.

dataDeletion.section1GoogleContent: When you disconnect Google Ads from the YoAi dashboard: Synchronization is stopped. Stored authorization data (token/refresh token) is revoked and/or invalidated. The related account mapping is queued for deletion.
dataDeletion.section1MetaContent: When you disconnect Meta from the YoAi dashboard: Synchronization is stopped. Authorization data is revoked and/or invalidated. The related account mapping is queued for deletion.
dataDeletion.section2Content: Integration tokens and authorization records; linked account mapping and connection metadata; report/cache summaries retained for product functionality.
dataDeletion.section3Content: After disconnect, data is removed from our systems within 90 days at the latest. You may contact us to request earlier deletion.
dataDeletion.section4Content: Email: info@yodijital.com | Subject: "YoAi Data Deletion Request" | Body: Your account email + the integration(s) you want deleted

cookiePolicy.title: Cookie Policy

intro.title: Introduction
intro.content: This Cookie Policy explains the cookies and similar technologies used on the platform provided by YO Dijital Medya Anonim Şirketi ('YoAi') at yoai.yodijital.com.

cookiePolicy.section1Content: Cookies are small text files placed on your browser by websites. They are used to remember your preferences, manage your session, and collect usage statistics.
cookiePolicy.section2Essential: Essential Cookies: Required for core platform functionality. Session management (session_id), user authentication, and security checks fall under this category. The platform cannot function properly without these cookies.
cookiePolicy.section2Functional: Functional Cookies: Used to remember your preferences such as language (NEXT_LOCALE), selected ad account, and dashboard settings.
cookiePolicy.section2Analytics: Analytics Cookies: Help us understand how the platform is used. Page views, click rates, and performance metrics are collected through these cookies.
cookiePolicy.section2ThirdParty: Third-Party Cookies: As part of Google Ads and Meta (Facebook) integrations, these platforms may set their own cookies. These cookies are subject to the respective platforms' privacy policies.
cookiePolicy.section3Content: Session cookies: Deleted when you close your browser. Persistent cookies: Stored for up to 30 days (e.g., session ID, user preferences). Third-party cookies: Varies according to the respective platform's policy.
cookiePolicy.section4Content: You can delete or block cookies through your browser settings. However, disabling essential cookies may negatively affect the platform's functionality. You can update your cookie preferences at any time through your browser settings.
cookiePolicy.section5Content: Our platform uses Cloudflare infrastructure. Cloudflare may set its own cookies (cf_clearance, __cf_bm, etc.) for bot protection and security verification. These cookies serve security purposes and are subject to Cloudflare's privacy policy.
cookiePolicy.section6Content: Cookie usage is carried out in accordance with the Turkish Personal Data Protection Law (KVKK) No. 6698 and the European Union General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR). Essential cookies are used under legitimate interest, while other cookies are used with your explicit consent.
cookiePolicy.section7Content: This Cookie Policy may be updated from time to time. The current version is always published on this page.

toasts.searchTermsAddedAsNegative: {count} search terms added as negative keywords

0.title: Campaign name and internal management
0.body: The campaign name is not shown to users; it is purely for internal management. Therefore the naming structure should be readable using date, channel, goal and variant logic. Correct naming directly improves reporting, filtering and in-team operational speed.

1.title: How the daily budget works
1.body: Google Ads handles the daily budget as an average daily spend rather than a hard fixed cap. On some days the system may spend more to capture high-traffic opportunities. Budget evaluation should be based on the period average, not a single day.

2.title: Choosing a bidding strategy
2.body: The bidding strategy must align with the campaign goal. Click-focused strategies suit traffic-oriented setups, while conversion-focused strategies make more sense for sales or lead-oriented ones. The wrong bidding strategy can make even a good ad inefficient.

3.title: Impact of Search Network Partners
3.body: The Search Network Partners option can let ads appear not only on Google Search but also on search sites partnered with Google. This setting can increase reach, but traffic quality may vary by campaign. It should not be left on blindly without separate performance checks.

4.title: Display Network scope
4.body: When the Display Network is enabled, ads can appear across a much wider inventory, including websites, apps and video content. While this expansion increases visibility, it may not bring traffic as warm as search intent. In Search campaigns this setting should be evaluated carefully against the goal.

0.title: The purpose of negative keywords
0.body: Negative keywords are used to exclude searches where you do not want your ads to appear. This reduces irrelevant traffic, protects the budget and raises conversion quality. It is a critical control layer, especially in campaigns using broad match.

1.title: It reduces wasted spend
1.body: Users with the wrong search intent rarely convert even when they click. Negative lists filter out such queries so spend is directed toward more targeted searches. The negative structure is therefore a budget defense mechanism rather than a performance booster.

2.title: Mind the match logic
2.body: Negative keywords do not work with the same logic as positive ones, so broad, phrase and exact match interpretations should not be assumed identical. Overly aggressive negatives can cut valuable traffic too. Before expanding the list, verify which searches genuinely need to be excluded.

3.title: Campaign or ad group level
3.body: When negatives are added at the campaign level, they affect all ad groups. When finer control is needed, separating them at the ad group level is healthier. For shared and recurring exclusions, a centralized list approach should be preferred.

4.title: Works together with the search terms report
4.body: The negative structure is not set up once and forgotten. Search terms should be reviewed regularly and new irrelevant queries added to the negative list. The best negatives come from real user queries, not guesses.

0.title: Location targeting basics
0.body: Location targeting determines in which geographic regions ads are shown. You can target by country, city, district, specific areas or radius. For businesses providing local services, this area directly affects cost efficiency.

1.title: Presence vs. interest distinction
1.body: In location settings, users being physically in the target region is not the same as showing interest in it. If this distinction is set up incorrectly, ads may also reach users outside the target region. This setting should be chosen carefully, especially in local service campaigns.

2.title: Excluded regions
2.body: Choosing only the regions to target is not enough; locations where impressions should not be served must also be excluded when needed. Regions you do not serve, low-quality areas or cities that scatter the budget can be added to the exclusion list. This control matters especially in broad geographic setups.

3.title: Using radius targeting
3.body: Radius targeting is highly functional for stores, clinics, service areas or businesses providing physical services. The user's distance to the business can significantly affect conversion likelihood. However, if the radius is set too wide, the local advantage is lost.

4.title: Location performance should be read
4.body: Location reports show which cities or regions perform more efficiently. Low-performing regions can be reduced, while bids or budget can be increased in strong ones. Location management is not just targeting but also an optimization area.

0.title: Why assets matter
0.body: Assets don't just make the ad look larger; they also provide extra information to the user. Assets such as sitelinks, callouts, phone, address or price can increase the ad's click potential. A strong asset structure uses the ad space more efficiently.

1.title: Using sitelinks
1.body: Sitelink assets can direct users straight to relevant subpages. This way a single ad serves multiple intents and the user reaches the right page faster. They are especially valuable for service, category and promotion pages.

2.title: Callouts and structured snippets
2.body: Callout-type assets are used to quickly highlight brand or service advantages. These fields are ideal for emphasizing differentiators such as price, experience, speed, scope, support or expertise. They should provide complementary information rather than repeat the ad copy.

3.title: Call and location assets
3.body: Phone- and address-focused assets perform strongly, especially in local and mobile-oriented campaigns. Letting the user call directly or see the business location shortens the decision time. However, these assets must align with the business's actual operations.

4.title: Asset performance should be tracked
4.body: Not all assets contribute equally. The visibility, engagement and conversion contribution of the assets in use should be reviewed regularly. Unnecessary or low-contribution assets should be cleaned up and strong ones expanded.

0.title: Real user language appears here
0.body: The search terms report shows the real user queries that triggered your ad. The keyword plan is theoretical, while this report shows the real intent in the field. That is why it is one of the most valuable sources for optimization.

1.title: New keyword opportunities
1.body: High-intent, converting queries can be spotted here and added to the keyword set. This moves the campaign into a more controlled and deliberate structure. Watching successful queries is not enough; they should be turned into structure.

2.title: A source for negatives
2.body: Irrelevant, weak or unwanted queries should be spotted in this report and converted into negative keywords. This improves traffic quality and reduces unnecessary cost. The search terms report and negative management should be considered together.

3.title: Match type analysis
3.body: Examining which queries arrive through which keyword logic reveals how controlled the match strategy is. Overly scattered queries can indicate that broad match remains aggressive. In that case the keyword architecture should be narrowed again.

4.title: Ad copy and landing page insight
4.body: The language users employ can also be used in ad headlines and landing pages. The best terms provide critical data not only for the keyword pool but also for the messaging strategy. This report is a gold mine for content optimization too.

0.title: Audience logic
0.body: Audience segments group users by interest, intent, behavior and demographic signals. This lets you manage the ad more intelligently based not only on what is searched but also on who is searching. It is especially important for separating higher-quality traffic.

1.title: Observation vs. targeting
1.body: Observation mode does not restrict the audience; it only lets you monitor performance. Targeting mode limits the ad to the selected segments only. If these two modes are confused, the campaign either narrows unnecessarily or stays broad without producing insight.

2.title: The power of remarketing
2.body: Website visitors, cart abandoners or users who took certain actions can be re-targeted with remarketing lists. This structure usually carries higher conversion potential than cold traffic. However, list quality and membership duration should be chosen strategically.

3.title: Segment data is used for optimization
3.body: Audience reports should be read to understand which segments produce business results, not which segments receive clicks. A high CTR does not always mean a good audience. Conversions, cost and quality should be evaluated together.

4.title: Demographics alone are not enough
4.body: Demographic signals such as age, gender or household income can be useful, but on their own they are often insufficient to build a strategy. They gain meaning when read together with behavior and intent signals. Blind demographic narrowing can cut volume unnecessarily.

0.title: The landing page is a continuation of the ad
0.body: After the user clicks the ad, the experience continues on the landing page. Therefore there should be no disconnect between the ad message and the page content. A strong ad can be wasted because of a weak page.

1.title: Relevance affects quality
1.body: If the landing page does not align with the ad copy and search intent, quality score and conversion rate suffer. If users see one promise and encounter different content, the bounce rate rises. Message match is the key factor here.

2.title: Speed and mobile experience are critical
2.body: Pages that load slowly or work poorly on mobile directly undermine ad performance. Users do not want to wait after clicking; delay lowers conversion probability. A landing page should not be considered healthy until a mobile-first test is done.

3.title: One page, one purpose
3.body: The landing page should be designed to move the user to the next step, not to scatter their attention. Too many options, weak hierarchy or cluttered content disrupt the decision process. Whatever the ad's goal is, that should also be the page's main action.

4.title: Performance signals should be monitored
4.body: On-page behaviors such as bounce, scroll, form completion, call click or WhatsApp click matter for interpreting ad quality. The problem is not always in the traffic; sometimes the page itself is the issue. Ad optimization and page optimization should proceed together.

0.title: Scheduling control
0.body: Ad scheduling helps control on which days and hours ads are shown. Not every hour produces the same quality of traffic. This area becomes critical especially in setups tied to sales, phone or operating hours.

1.title: Serving according to working hours
1.body: A user's likelihood of converting is closely tied to the hours when the business can respond. In sectors requiring calls, WhatsApp or quick replies, off-hours traffic is often weak. Ad scheduling should align with the workflow.

2.title: Hourly efficiency differences
2.body: Some hours bring high volume while others can produce a higher conversion rate. This difference should be read from reports and the bidding or serving plan adjusted accordingly. Scheduling is not just an on-off setting but an efficiency filter.

3.title: Device and time should be read together
3.body: The same time slot can perform differently on mobile versus desktop. Therefore scheduling data is more meaningful when interpreted together with device performance. A one-dimensional reading is often misleading.

4.title: Don't make hard calls on thin data
4.body: Ad-time decisions should not be made on very short data ranges. A few days of data can be misleading, especially in low-volume campaigns. For a healthier decision, act on a meaningful period of data.

0.title: The role of keywords
0.body: Keywords determine on which searches your ad appears. This directly shapes the campaign's traffic character. Broad match provides the widest reach but can trigger on irrelevant searches; phrase and exact match offer more controlled serving.

1.title: Match types and behavior
1.body: Phrase match preserves the exact order for more targeted serving. Exact match triggers only on the exact search or very close variants. Keywords with a high quality score appear in higher positions at a lower cost. The match strategy should align with the campaign goal.

2.title: Performance-focused management
2.body: Keyword management is not just adding and removing. Low-performing keywords can be narrowed and bids increased on strong ones. When read together with the search terms report, the keyword set can be continuously optimized.

deploy.description: Each step runs independently; if one fails the others continue.

deploy.snippetTitle: Add the GTM Code to Your Site

nextjs.step1: Open app/layout.tsx (App Router) or pages/_document.tsx (Pages Router) in your project.
nextjs.step2: Paste the HEAD code inside the <head> tag, as high as possible. You can also use the GoogleTagManager component from the @next/third-parties package.
nextjs.step3: Paste the BODY code right after the opening <body> tag.
nextjs.step4: Deploy the site and verify the tags fire using Preview mode in Google Tag Manager.

wordpress.step1: In the WordPress admin, go to Plugins > Add New and install a code-insertion plugin such as WPCode (Insert Headers and Footers).
wordpress.step2: Paste the HEAD code into the plugin's Header field.
wordpress.step3: Paste the BODY code into the Body (opening) field; if the plugin has no such field, the Footer field also works.
wordpress.step4: Save, clear your cache plugin's cache if you use one, and verify with GTM Preview mode.

shopify.step1: In the Shopify admin, open Online Store > Themes > ⋯ > Edit code.
shopify.step2: In layout/theme.liquid, paste the HEAD code right below the <head> tag.
shopify.step3: Paste the BODY code right after the opening <body> tag and save.
shopify.step4: Checkout pages are restricted by Shopify; use Settings > Customer events (Pixels) for the purchase event. Verify with GTM Preview.

webflow.step1: In Webflow, open Site Settings > Custom Code (a paid site plan is required for custom code).
webflow.step2: Paste the HEAD code into the Head Code field.
webflow.step3: Paste the BODY code into the Footer Code field (Webflow injects it at the end of the body; sufficient for tracking).
webflow.step4: Click Save Changes and Publish the site; verify with GTM Preview mode.

other.step1: Send these two code snippets to the person or agency managing your website.
other.step2: The HEAD code must be added inside the <head> tag on every page, as high as possible.
other.step3: The BODY code must be added right after the opening <body> tag on every page.
other.step4: After installation, verify the tags work using Preview mode in Google Tag Manager.

pricing.faqSubtitle: Quick answers to the questions you may have.

faq.q: Can I change or cancel my plan anytime?
faq.a: Yes. You can upgrade, downgrade or cancel your plan at any time. There is no commitment.

faq.q: Can I add more ad accounts later?
faq.a: Yes. Every plan scales with the number of ad accounts; you can add new accounts whenever you need them.

faq.q: What is the difference between monthly and yearly billing?
faq.a: When you choose yearly billing, a 30% discount applies to all plans.

faq.q: Which ad platforms do you support?
faq.a: You can create and manage your Google Ads and Meta (Facebook, Instagram) ads from a single panel.

faq.q: Do I need a credit card to start the free trial?
faq.a: No. You don't need to enter any credit card details to start your free trial.

faq.q: Who is the Enterprise plan for?
faq.a: It is designed for agencies and large teams managing 7 or more ad accounts, with custom pricing based on your needs.

helpCenter.title: Help Center
helpCenter.subtitle: Frequently asked questions and support channels
helpCenter.contactDesc: If you couldn't find an answer, reach our support team by email. We'll get back to you as soon as possible.

platform.q: What is YoAi and what can I do with it?
platform.a: YoAi is an AI-powered marketing platform that lets you manage your Meta and Google ads from a single panel. It includes ad management, strategy planning, optimization recommendations, audience building, design generation, reporting, SEO analysis, CRM and email marketing modules.

connectAccounts.q: How do I connect my ad accounts?
connectAccounts.a: You can securely connect your Meta and Google accounts from the Integration page using official authorization (OAuth). YoAi never sees your password; authorization happens directly on the platform itself.

recommendations.q: Do YoAlgorithm recommendations change my account automatically?
recommendations.a: No. YoAlgorithm analyzes your ad account and offers improvement recommendations; nothing is applied to your ad account until you approve it.

creditsVsSubscription.q: What is the difference between a subscription and AI credits?
creditsVsSubscription.a: A subscription gives access to modules such as Strategy, Optimization, YoAlgorithm and SEO. AI credits are spent on usage-based AI operations such as design generation. You can manage both from the Subscription page.

language.q: How do I change the interface language?
language.a: Open the profile menu at the bottom left and choose Türkçe or English from the language option.

dataSecurity.q: Are my data and connection permissions safe?
dataSecurity.a: Your platform connection keys are stored encrypted and used only for the operations you have authorized. See the Privacy Policy page for details.

subscriptionManage.q: How do I manage my subscription?
subscriptionManage.a: You can view, upgrade or change your plan from the Subscription page. Your invoices are available on the My Invoices page.
